title: BEM-915 protocol record
objective:
Create file governance/reports/BEM915_TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_INTERFACE_PROTOCOL.md with content # BEM-915 TELEGRAM OPERATOR INTERFACE PROTOCOL
Status: TELEGRAM_PROTOCOL_APPROVED_WITH_OPERATOR_GATES
Date: 2026-05-30
Purpose: update the BEM-914 system improvement protocol with a dedicated Telegram Operator Interface layer.
Reason:
- Operator screenshot shows the bot currently behaves like passive hourly notifier and does not provide effective task submission, role-based reports or gate decisions.
- Deno health confirms Telegram webhook service exists, but user-facing workflow is insufficient.
Worker agreement:
- Request: governance/audit/mailbox/to_curator/BEM915_TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_INTERFACE_PROTOCOL_REQUEST.md
- Worker review: governance/audit/internal/results/BEM915_WORKER_TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_INTERFACE_REVIEW.md
- Curator response: governance/audit/mailbox/from_curator/BEM915_TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_INTERFACE_PROTOCOL_RESPONSE.md
Protocol additions:
1. Telegram becomes primary operator-facing control channel.
2. Commands: /status, /task, /approve, /reject, /gate, /report, /help, /cancel.
3. Every incoming operator message becomes structured Telegram task envelope with trace_id, task_id, target object, priority, classification, policy decision, route, proof path and receipt status.
4. Routing supports Curator, Director, General Director and Worker.
5. Reports are split by role: Curator operational report, Director planning/rules report, General Director strategic/gate report.
6. Bot must provide delivery receipts, correlation IDs, error states and Telegram E2E selftest.
7. Telegram integration uses existing managed channel, policy gate, dispatch queue and proof policy.
Operator gates:
- real bot token/secrets;
- production webhook changes;
- issue #31 writes;
- schedule triggers;
- always-on daemon model;
- real pilot/client/scope/metric selection.
Local operator artifacts:
- BEM915_protocol_telegram_operator_interface_ru.docx
- BEM915_protocol_telegram_operator_interface_ru.zip
QA: DOCX rendered to 6 pages; no edge clipping detected.
No issue comments.
